title: koeleria spicata subsp. geghamensis
intro: Koeleria spicata subsp. geghamensis, also known as Gegham's Koeleria, is a subspecies of a perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to Armenia and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues such as colds, fever, and respiratory problems, thanks to its expectorant and bronchodilatory effects. Koeleria spicata subsp. geghamensis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some traditional medicine practices, it is used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
